[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.67) — 13 imports target "web-dashboard"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.33) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.67 exceeds the 0.6 threshold, but this is a classification artifact: viewer components are connected to web-dashboard while build scripts have no imports, inflating the ratio.
[observation] [info] Developer hints confirm viewer files (elapsed-time.ts, route-state.ts, tick-timer.ts, task-audit.ts, token-usage.ts, usage/constants.ts) should be pinned to web-dashboard to resolve the mixed-purpose zone.
[observation] [info] build.js and dev.js are executable build runner entrypoints, not static config — they belong in a web-package-config or web-dashboard zone rather than being classified as [config] archetypes.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "web-viewer-utilities" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] This is the only zone in the monorepo with BOTH high coupling (0.67) AND low cohesion (0.33) simultaneously — the fragile-zone risk profile. All other zones with non-zero coupling maintain high cohesion. Apply viewer file pins immediately to collapse coupling toward zero and restore meaningful cohesion measurement.
[suggestion] [info] Zone name 'web-viewer-utilities' will be misleading after viewer files are pinned to web-dashboard. Plan a rename to 'web-build-config' post-pin so the ID accurately reflects the residual build infrastructure content.
[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Web Viewer Utilities" (web-viewer-utilities) has critical risk (score: 0.67, cohesion: 0.33, coupling: 0.67) — requires refactoring before new feature development
[relationship] [info] All 13 outbound coupling imports target web-dashboard; there are no cross-package imports, confirming the coupling metric is a zone classification artifact rather than an architectural defect.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Seven viewer UI files (elapsed-time.ts, use-tick.ts, tick-timer.ts, route-state.ts, task-audit.ts, lazy-children.ts, listener-lifecycle.ts) are stranded in a build-infrastructure zone. This misclassification causes both the viewer cohesion score and the build-zone coupling score to be meaningless. Zone pins must be applied to migrate all packages/web/src/viewer/** files to web-dashboard before zone metrics can be trusted.
[anti-pattern] [warning] build.js and dev.js are assigned the [config] archetype instead of [entrypoint]. Archetype-driven tooling will treat them as passive configuration rather than executable process roots, leading to incorrect change-impact and dead-code analysis.

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.33) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.67) — 13 imports target "web-dashboard"
- Low cohesion (0.33) is expected and documented: build/config files (build.js, dev.js, package.json) do not import each other, dragging cohesion down despite the viewer files being cohesive among themselves.
- The viewer entry points (elapsed-time.ts, tick-timer.ts, route-state.ts, task-audit.ts, token-usage.ts) all belong in the web-dashboard zone per developer hints; zone pins should be applied to migrate them and leave this zone as pure build infrastructure.
- High coupling (0.67) reflects the viewer files' natural connections to the broader web-dashboard zone — once pinned correctly, coupling for the remaining build infrastructure should drop to near zero.
- Coupling of 0.67 exceeds the 0.6 threshold, but this is a classification artifact: viewer components are connected to web-dashboard while build scripts have no imports, inflating the ratio.
- Developer hints confirm viewer files (elapsed-time.ts, route-state.ts, tick-timer.ts, task-audit.ts, token-usage.ts, usage/constants.ts) should be pinned to web-dashboard to resolve the mixed-purpose zone.
- build.js and dev.js are executable build runner entrypoints, not static config — they belong in a web-package-config or web-dashboard zone rather than being classified as [config] archetypes.
- Zone "web-viewer-utilities" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The zone's high coupling (0.67) is entirely directed at web-dashboard — once viewer files are pinned away, the residual build infrastructure (build.js, dev.js) will have zero coupling, making this a pure config zone.
- All 13 outbound coupling imports target web-dashboard; there are no cross-package imports, confirming the coupling metric is a zone classification artifact rather than an architectural defect.
- The zone contains two categorically different concerns — viewer UI components (elapsed-time.ts, use-tick.ts, tick-timer.ts, route-state.ts, task-audit.ts, lazy-children.ts, listener-lifecycle.ts) and build infrastructure (build.js, dev.js, package.json, tsconfig.json) — making it structurally impossible to apply meaningful cohesion/coupling thresholds to either concern independently. Health metrics for this zone are noise until the viewer files are pinned to web-dashboard.
- build.js and dev.js carry the [config] archetype but are executable build runner entrypoints, not static configuration. Any tooling that uses archetypes to drive decisions (e.g. change-impact scoring, dead-code detection) will misclassify these files as passive data rather than active process launchers.
- Seven viewer UI files (elapsed-time.ts, use-tick.ts, tick-timer.ts, route-state.ts, task-audit.ts, lazy-children.ts, listener-lifecycle.ts) are stranded in a build-infrastructure zone. This misclassification causes both the viewer cohesion score and the build-zone coupling score to be meaningless. Zone pins must be applied to migrate all packages/web/src/viewer/** files to web-dashboard before zone metrics can be trusted.
- build.js and dev.js are assigned the [config] archetype instead of [entrypoint]. Archetype-driven tooling will treat them as passive configuration rather than executable process roots, leading to incorrect change-impact and dead-code analysis.
- Zone name 'web-viewer-utilities' will become a misnomer once viewer files are pinned away — the residual build infrastructure (build.js, dev.js, package.json, tsconfig.json) warrants renaming to 'web-build-config' or 'web-package-config' to reflect its true post-pin purpose.
- This is the only zone in the entire monorepo that simultaneously exhibits high coupling (0.67) AND low cohesion (0.33) — the canonical fragile-zone risk pattern. All other zones with non-zero coupling maintain high cohesion; this zone is the sole structural outlier matching the risk profile.
- Zone name 'web-viewer-utilities' will be misleading after viewer files are pinned to web-dashboard. Plan a rename to 'web-build-config' post-pin so the ID accurately reflects the residual build infrastructure content.
- This is the only zone in the monorepo with BOTH high coupling (0.67) AND low cohesion (0.33) simultaneously — the fragile-zone risk profile. All other zones with non-zero coupling maintain high cohesion. Apply viewer file pins immediately to collapse coupling toward zero and restore meaningful cohesion measurement.
